channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Arrested Philippine ex-President Duterte to face legacy of thousands killed in drug crackdown
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine ex-President Rodrigo Duterte, who left a savage legacy over his deadly anti-drugs crackdown, was arrested on a warrant issued by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity for the thousands killed in a war he waged against drugs in a political
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the Menendez brothers' bid for freedom
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles says he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez, brothers who have spent more than 30 years in prison for killing their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County, Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine will propose a limited ceasefire during talks with the US in Saudi Arabia, officials say
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — A Ukrainian delegation set to meet with America’s top diplomat in Saudi Arabia about ending the 3-year war with Russia will propose a ceasefire covering the Black Sea and long-range missile strikes, as well as the release of prisoners, two senior Ukrainian officials said
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US-Canada products are 99% tariff-free: Canadian ambassador to US
item.description: Canada's ambassador to the United States said 99% of products traded between the two nations are tariff-free.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security to again recoup 100% of overpayments
item.description: The Office of the Chief Actuary estimated the withholding rate increase could save about $7 billion over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Arrest of Palestinian activist stirs questions about protections for students and green card holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The arrest of a Palestinian activist who helped organize campus protests of the war in Gaza has sparked questions about whether foreign students and green card holders are protected against being deported from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LA district attorney says he won’t support resentencing the Menendez brothers because they lied
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The district attorney of Los Angeles County said Monday that he does not support the resentencing of Lyle and Erik Menendez because the brothers have repeatedly lied about why they killed their parents at their Beverly Hills home in 1989.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Musk eyes Social Security and benefit programs for cuts while claiming widespread fraud
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk pushed debunked theories about Social Security on Monday while describing federal benefit programs as rife with fraud, suggesting they will be a primary target in his crusade to reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 victims of Pennsylvania plane crash were taken to a burn unit for treatment, hospital says
item.description: LITITZ, Pa. (AP) — Three of the five family members injured in a single-engine plane crash in Pennsylvania after the pilot reported an open door on the aircraft were sent to a burn center for treatment, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Mexico expels alleged US child kidnapper
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – An American who allegedly took his two young children to Mexico without consent and then demanded ransom from the mother is back in Texas to face kidnapping charges.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department's Office for Civil Rights is known best for enforcing the right to disability services across America’s schools. But under President Donald Trump, it’s taking a frontline role in his political battles.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI made its way to vineyards. Here's how the technology is helping make your wine
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When artificial intelligence-backed tractors became available to vineyards, Tom Gamble wanted to be an early adopter. He knew there would be a learning curve, but Gamble decided the technology was worth figuring out. The third-generation farmer bought one autonomous tractor.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge awards nearly $2M after ruling FBI agent was negligent in fatal shooting of abducted Texas man
item.description: A federal judge awarded nearly $2 million in damages after ruling that an FBI agent was negligent when he shot and killed a kidnapped Texas man during a botched rescue effort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona prisoner set to be executed next week passes up chance to ask for reprieve
item.description: A prisoner scheduled to be executed in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years has passed up a chance to ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: Jacques Billeaud, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mississippi pastor, deacon charged with embezzling church funds
item.description: Pastor Stricjavvar Strickland and Deacon Armond Barnes have been charged with embezzlement of nearly $90,000 in church funds after an investigation by the Covington County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Sethanie Smith, WGN 9

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why these 2 states didn't 'spring forward' to daylight saving time this morning
item.description: You may have been groggy this morning thanks to our clocks “springing forward” an hour overnight. Unless, of course, you live in either of the two states that don’t observe daylight saving time.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Do you need fluoride after a teeth cleaning? Dental experts weigh in
item.description: Dentists and hygienists often propose fluoride treatments to patients in the dental chair to prevent cavities and strengthen teeth. But the relatively simple procedure – which involves applying a varnish, gel or foam directly to the teeth – isn’t always covered by insurance, especially for adults.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tatum scores 40, Celtics beat Lakers 111-101 after James leaves late with groin injury
item.description: Jayson Tatum had 40 points, 12 rebounds, and eight assists, and the Boston Celtics held off a late rally to beat the Los Angeles Lakers 111-101 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why Chi-Chi’s restaurants are returning 2 decades after their closure
item.description: While multiple casual dining chains are looking to reduce their footprints this year, a once-beloved but long-shuttered brand is preparing for a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Bannon: DOGE 'waste, fraud, abuse’ cuts not enough to curb inflation
item.description: Bannon, Trump’s former adviser and critic of Musk, has condemned the tech giant's motives and methods.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy arrested for assault in Texas
item.description: On Friday, Chiefs star rookie Xavier Worthy, 21, was arrested in Williamson County, Texas after investigators said he assaulted a family member or person in his household by "impeding their breathing or circulation.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Watch the moon turn red during a total lunar eclipse in March
item.description: A total lunar eclipse will flush the moon red Thursday night into Friday morning across the Western Hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit aims to strike down LGBTQ antidiscrimination protections in Pennsylvania
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Two public school districts and several parents have sued Pennsylvania in a bid to undo antidiscrimination protections for gay and transgender people, saying the state's two-year-old regulation is illegal because it goes beyond what lawmakers intended or allowed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Louisiana student died after being punched in hazing ritual, police say
item.description: Baton Rouge police confirmed Southern University student Caleb Wilson died after being punched in the chest multiple times during a hazing ritual. One arrest has been made, and more suspects are expected.
item.dc:creator: Keymonte Avery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexican cartel leader’s son is sentenced to life in prison for role in major drug trafficking plot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Mexican drug cartel boss was sentenced Friday to life in prison for helping his father run one of the country’s largest and most violent narcotics trafficking organizations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States sue President Trump’s administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Maryland and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies, contending President Donald Trump's administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CEO of Jack Daniel's responds after whiskey pulled from Canada's shelves
item.description: The Distilled Spirits Council of the United States applauded President Donald Trump’s decision to suspend tariffs one day after some Canadian provinces started pulling U.S. liquor, including Jack Daniel's, from shelves.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US military's mini space shuttle returns to Earth after orbiting for 434 days on a secret mission
item.description: The U.S. military's classified mini space shuttle returned to Earth on Friday after circling the world for 434 days.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump offers pathway to citizenship for South African farmers, families
item.description: President Trump has offered an expedited pathway to U.S. citizenship to South African farmers, calling their treatment in the country "terrible" and citing property laws that impose "unjust racial discrimination" against white Afrikaner farmers.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Family on cruise ship gets lost luggage back after weeks of waiting, but half of their personal items are missing
item.description: Frustrations continue for Jason and Tammy Merritt after waiting a month for their lost luggage, which ended up being on-board the Carnival Cruise Line the entire time.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, WFLA, NBC
